name: Mackerel with pistachio and cardamom salsa
intro: This is a super impressive (but secretly very easy) summer starter. It also works

ingredients.item: cardamom pods (or ½ teaspoon of ground cardamom) salt
mackerel fillets, skin on and pinbones removed (9¼ oz/260g)
shelled pistachios, lightly toasted and roughly chopped
limes:  nely zest 1 to get 1 tsp, then juice to get 2 tsp; cut the second lime into wedges, to serve
green chile, seeded and  nely chopped

method: Using the  at side of a large knife, crush the cardamom pods to release the seeds. Transfer the seeds to a pestle and mortar and crush them nely; you
should have about ½ tsp. Discard the pods. If starting with ground cardamom you don’t need to do this.
Mix a pinch of cardamom (about ⅛ tsp) with a pinch of salt. Rub this on both sides of the  sh and set aside until ready to fry.
Finely grate the ginger, then press the pulp through a sieve (with a bowl underneath); you should get 1 tsp of juice. Discard the pulp and set the liquid aside.
Using a fork (or a small whisk, if you have one), whip the cream until stiff.
Switch to using a spatula and fold in the sour cream, ginger juice, and a small pinch of salt, so you have a soft cream. Keep in the fridge until needed.
Combine the cilantro and basil with the pistachios, the remaining ⅛ tsp of cardamom, the lime zest, lime juice, chile, 4 tsp/20ml of the oil, and ⅛ tsp salt. Set aside.

When ready to serve, put the remaining 1 tbsp plus 2 tsp of oil into a large frying pan and place over high heat. Once very hot, add the mackerel  llets, skin side down (the skin should sizzle), and fry for 2 minutes. Press the  llets with a  sh spatula as they cook, to prevent the skin from curling up. Once the skin is crisp and golden brown, turn over and fry for 1 minute, until golden brown.
Transfer the mackerel to four individual plates. Spoon one-quarter of the ginger cream and the pistachio and cardamom salsa next to each llet. Serve hot, with a wedge of lime.
